description:
A SINGLE UNINTERRUPTED PANORAMIC PAINTING of one continuous interior space inside THE SAME ANTIQUE-DEALER'S RESIDENCE as the cabinet/bureau scene of this game — same warm Art Déco palette, same museum-catalog rendering — combining a STORAGE area on the LEFT THIRD and a CRAFTSMAN'S ATELIER on the CENTER + RIGHT THIRDS, sharing one single tall room. Rendered as ONE flowing seamless mural with absolutely NO vertical lines dividing the image, NO panels, NO image seams, NO black borders between any parts — strictly forbidden to split the image visually into sections; the result must read as a single oil-painted scroll-like view of the same room.

ABSOLUTELY CRITICAL compositional constraint: this panorama will be viewed on a smartphone that swipes horizontally and snaps to THREE viewing windows (0–33%, 33–66%, 66–100% of the width). For clean swipes, NO furniture, NO door, NO window, NO rack, NO shelving unit, NO architectural feature MAY CROSS OR STRADDLE the vertical positions at exactly 33% or 66%. Every major object must be contained ENTIRELY within ONE of the three thirds. The areas around 33% and 66% (roughly 31–35% and 64–68%) must contain ONLY plain continuous back wall + floor — these are the 'breathing zones' for swipe boundaries.

Architecture: the viewer looks into a warm Art Déco interior at eye-level. The BACK WALL runs unbroken across the full width (0% to 100%), warm aged plaster (cream/sepia tone, NOT industrial brick), with occasional exposed vertical wooden framing posts. A LEFT SIDE WALL angles forward at the far left (0–8%), a RIGHT SIDE WALL angles forward at the far right (92–100%). ONE continuous WARM WOOD PLANK FLOOR (parquet vintage) runs unbroken across the bottom. The ceiling is high (~4m), with one continuous exposed dark-wood ceiling beam across the top.

Feature details by horizontal position (0% = left edge, 100% = right edge):

• 0–8% — LEFT SIDE WALL angles forward, plain warm plaster, empty.

• 7–28% — SECTION 1 (STORAGE LEFT): a tall INDUSTRIAL METAL STORAGE RACK (rayonnage métallique) standing AGAINST the BACK WALL, PARALLEL to the wall, front face square toward the viewer. Industrial vintage construction: dark forest-green painted STEEL ANGLE-IRON UPRIGHTS (riveted), with FIVE horizontal metal shelves stacked at evenly spaced heights from near the floor up to ~2m. Most shelves are EMPTY (naked patinated metal, ready to receive composited items later) EXCEPT for a small touch of decoration: 2 plain CARDBOARD STORAGE BOXES (small, closed, brown) sitting on the SECOND-FROM-TOP shelf, slightly off-center, and 1 small wooden CRATE on the TOP shelf. Three lower/central shelves remain entirely empty. Fully contained within Section 1 (left edge ≥10%, right edge ≤28%, comfortable margin before the 31% start of the breathing zone). Faint surface patina, very subtle wear; NO dust on the floor or shelves here (storage side stays clean).

• 31–35% — BREATHING ZONE: ONLY plain warm-plaster back wall + clean wood floor. The 33% mark perfectly empty.

• 36–63% — SECTION 2 (ATELIER CENTER): on the BACK WALL, a substantial PROFESSIONAL ARTISAN WORKBENCH (établi de menuisier) centered around 50% horizontal, fully contained within Section 2 (left edge ≥38%, right edge ≤62%). Real workshop workbench: ~1.5m wide, ~85–90cm tall (top at hip-level for a standing adult), THICK worn-beech/oak top (8–10cm thick slab), four heavy square legs with sturdy lower stretchers, and a CAST-IRON BENCH VICE (étau en fonte) bolted to the front-left corner of the top with a visible turning handle. The workbench top is COMPLETELY EMPTY apart from the integrated vice — bare worn wood ready for composited items.

• Just above the workbench, a BEAUTIFUL TALL WINDOW centered around 50%, fully contained within Section 2 (left edge ≥38%, right edge ≤62%). The window is a generous WOODEN-FRAMED window with rich wood tones: the main body is ~1.3m wide and ~1.5m tall, with the sill resting about 15cm above the workbench top, divided into 3 vertical × 3 horizontal panes of clear glass by simple straight wooden mullions. Crowning the top of the window is a SPECTACULAR ART NOUVEAU STAINED GLASS IMPOSTE (vitrail style Art Nouveau): a decorative leaded-glass transom panel about 30cm tall, depicting a STYLIZED BLACKSMITH'S ANVIL (enclume) as the central motif, framed by curving organic Art Nouveau outlines and stylized leaves/tendrils. The stained glass uses warm jewel tones (amber, soft forest green, warm cream, hints of brass-gold), with visible lead came outlining each shape. Sunlight glows softly through the stained glass, casting faint colored highlights onto the workbench top below.

• Below the windowsill on the interior side, a WIDE WOODEN WINDOW LEDGE / TABLETTE (~30cm deep) protrudes generously into the room, polished warm wood. On the tablette, two TASTEFUL DECORATIONS: on the LEFT side of the tablette, a TERRACOTTA CLAY FLOWERPOT holding a small bouquet of warm-toned flowers (geraniums or marigolds, ochre/red petals); on the RIGHT side of the tablette, a TURNED-WOODEN CYLINDRICAL POT holding 4–5 PAINT BRUSHES with worn wooden handles and tips of various sizes. The decorations are placed close to the edges of the tablette so the center remains uncluttered.

• Through the clear panes (around the imposte), we see a SMALL CULTIVATED GARDEN (petit jardin): rosebushes in full bloom against a low stone garden wall, climbing roses on a wooden trellis, a paved garden path with a clay flowerpot or two on the path, warm afternoon sunlight, cozy enclosed feel.

• The floor immediately below this workbench shows FAINT DUST + a hint of WOOD SHAVINGS — the atelier half has working dust.

• 64–68% — BREATHING ZONE: ONLY plain back wall + faintly dusty wood floor. The 66% mark perfectly empty.

• 69–95% — SECTION 3 (ATELIER RIGHT): on the BACK WALL, a COMPOSITE WORKBENCH-AND-SHELF FURNITURE PIECE (meuble composé : établi + étagère), forming ONE single integrated wooden craft furniture that runs along the back wall and the angled right side wall, fully contained within Section 3.

  Left portion (≈70–85% horizontal) — the WORKBENCH portion: a real workshop workbench, ~1.1m wide, ~85–90cm tall, thick worn-oak top, sturdy square legs with lower stretchers, same construction language as the central one. Top is COMPLETELY EMPTY (no integrated vice this time — this one is for assembly/light work). The workbench top sits at hip-level.

  Right portion (≈86–95% horizontal) — the WOODEN SHELF UNIT (étagère en bois) BUILT AS A SEAMLESS EXTENSION of the workbench: the workbench top extends seamlessly to the right, where a vertical wooden hutch RISES UP from the workbench top to nearly the ceiling beam. The hutch is built of the same warm wood: two vertical wooden side panels (left side panel acts as the dividing structure between the open workbench area on the left and the shelved area on the right; right side panel sits against the angled right wall), enclosing FOUR horizontal wooden shelves at evenly spaced heights from just above the workbench top up to about 2m. The shelf unit and workbench share the SAME wood, finish, and construction style, reading as ONE composed piece of artisan furniture, NOT two separate items.

  Decoration on the wooden shelves: just 2 small craft elements scattered across the 4 shelves — on the SECOND shelf from the bottom, 2 small RECTANGULAR WOODEN STORAGE BOXES (different sizes, plain, closed); on the TOP shelf, 1 small terracotta crock or jar. The other 2 shelves remain entirely EMPTY (ready for composited items later).

  Fully contained within Section 3 (left edge ≥70%, right edge ≤95%, clear of the breathing zone).

• The floor immediately below this composite furniture also shows FAINT DUST + small wood shavings — atelier dust continues here.

• 95–100% — RIGHT SIDE WALL angles forward beyond the composite furniture, plain warm plaster with one exposed wooden timber post.

Lighting: soft warm directional light from the upper-left, plus the daylight + colored highlights from the Section 2 window with its stained-glass anvil imposte. The ATELIER half (Sections 2 and 3) is warmer and brighter, with soft pools of daylight on the floor and faint dust motes visible in slanted light beams. The STORAGE side (Section 1) is cleaner and slightly cooler. Cohesive Art Déco rendering: cream parchment paper texture, sepia ink line-art, soft forest green wash, brass and warm wood tones, subtle paper grain — IDENTICAL style to the bureau cabinet panorama of this same game (NOT industrial photography, NOT modern warehouse, NOT realistic 3D). No people, no text, no captions, no signatures, no watermark.

CRITICAL FINAL CONSTRAINTS:
(a) STYLE MUST MATCH the Bureau cabinet panorama of this same game EXACTLY — vintage Art Déco ink-and-wash, sepia + forest green, cream parchment, warm wood tones, soft museum-catalog rendering. NOT industrial photo, NOT modern warehouse.
(b) NO furniture, rack, window, shelving unit, or stained-glass imposte crosses or straddles the vertical positions at 33% or 66% — those positions are strictly plain back wall + floor.
(c) Storage rack (Section 1) stands AGAINST the back wall, PARALLEL to it (front face square toward viewer).
(d) Section 2's window has an ART NOUVEAU STAINED GLASS IMPOSTE depicting a stylized ANVIL (enclume) in warm jewel tones, framed by curving organic Art Nouveau outlines and stylized plant tendrils. Below the imposte, clear glass panes divided by simple wooden mullions.
(e) WIDE WOODEN TABLETTE under the Section 2 window (~30cm deep), with TASTEFUL DECORATION: clay flowerpot with warm-toned flowers on the LEFT, wooden cylindrical brush pot with paintbrushes on the RIGHT. Center of the tablette stays uncluttered.
(f) Section 3 features a COMPOSITE FURNITURE (one integrated piece): a workbench whose right end seamlessly extends UPWARD into a tall wooden shelf hutch (4 shelves), reading as ONE single composed wooden artisan furniture. NOT two separate pieces.
(g) Workbenches are REAL workshop workbenches at standard working height (~85–90cm = hip-level for a standing adult), thick wooden top, sturdy square legs with lower stretchers. The central one (Section 2) has a cast-iron bench vice on the front-left corner.
(h) DUST + wood shavings ONLY on the ATELIER half (Sections 2 and 3 floor). STOCKAGE half (Section 1) floor is CLEAN, no dust, no shavings.
(i) Decorations on the panorama are minimal and tasteful: 2 cardboard boxes + 1 wooden crate on the storage rack; clay flowerpot with flowers + wooden brush pot on the window tablette; 2 small wooden boxes + 1 terracotta jar on the composite shelf unit. Most shelves and workbench tops remain EMPTY (so the player can composite items there later).
(j) The garden visible through the Section 2 window is a SMALL CULTIVATED GARDEN (petit jardin) — rosebushes, low stone wall, climbing roses, paved path, clay pots. NOT vast countryside.
(k) No transparent areas — PNG is fully opaque. No vertical seams, no panel borders, no black bars.
